cross_scene_arcs.title: Final Testament to Mrs. Rand
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Rand insists on bequeathing all assets to Mrs. Rand, expressing deep love and trust, culminating in his deathbed will.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: In hospital, finalizing will with attorneys
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Will documented with everything to Mrs. Rand, emotional declaration made

cross_scene_arcs.title: Friendship with Chance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Rand introduces Chance as a dear friend and later finds his spirits lifted by Chance's presence, deepening their bond.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sees Chance as a friend and introduces him to the President
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Spirits improved due to Chance, acknowledging terminal illness
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Health continues to decline despite emotional uplift

cross_scene_arcs.title: Health Decline and Acceptance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Rand progresses from disclosing his impending death to being under medical care and finally on his deathbed, finalizing his affairs.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Acknowledges mortality and encourages resistance to despair
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Death is imminent, but preparations are made

cross_scene_arcs.title: Advisor to Others
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Rand continues to advise and care for others, from encouraging Chauncey to advising the President, despite his illness.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Actively encourages Chauncey to fight despair
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Advises the President against temporary measures, remaining influential
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether his advice will be followed, but he fulfills his role

cross_scene_arcs.title: Departure from the Old Man's Household
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chance remains detached as Louise reports the Old Man's decline and death; after the death, Louise softens and advises Chance on companionship before saying goodbye, leading to his departure from the garden.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chance detached in dining room, Old Man alive but worsening.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chance resumes gardening after Louise leaves, effectively ending his time in the house.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Identity and Displacement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chance is questioned by lawyers about his residence, insists he is the gardener but has no documentation; subsequently, he finds himself locked out of the townhouse in a decaying ghetto and walks away.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chance in dining room under questioning by lawyers.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chance walking left on the sidewalk in the decaying ghetto.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His lack of documentation and uncertain residence.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Encounter with the Rand World
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chance is injured by a limo, receives medical care from Jeffrey and Dr. Allenby, declines a legal claim, and accepts an invitation to dinner with the terminally ill Benjamin Rand, marking his entry into the Rand household.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chance injured on the street after being bumped by a limo.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chance has accepted Rand's dinner invitation and is under care at the Rand estate.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Public Emergence and Influence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Chance is introduced to the President, makes puzzling comments, and later appears on television where his literal interpretation of a garden metaphor gains applause, elevating his public profile.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chance in the library with Rand and the President, causing puzzlement.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chance gains national media attention from the TV appearance.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Aftermath and New Role
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After Rand's death, Chance expresses love for Eve and agrees to care for her; he is later found by Eve on a hillside, and they reunite with Allenby, departing together.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Chance at Rand's deathbed, agreeing to care for Eve.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chance reunited with Eve and Allenby, departing in a limousine.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Providing Medical Aid
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eve insists on taking the injured Chance to a doctor, arranges for her family physician, and introduces herself.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eve sees Chance's leg injury and offers help.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Chance is being taken to Eve's home for examination.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Chance's identity is misunderstood as 'Chauncey Gardiner'.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Marriage and Widowhood
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eve handles her husband's request to meet Chance, discusses his impending death, and becomes a widow when Rand dies.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eve is married to Benjamin Rand.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eve is a widow with Chance tasked as her caretaker.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: How Eve will manage after Rand's death.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Unrequited Love
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eve's interest in Chance grows from clarification about Louise to declarations of love, but her advances are not reciprocated.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eve misunderstands Chance's relationship with Louise.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eve's intimacy attempt is ignored by Chance.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Finding Chance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eve expresses concern for Chance's safety and successfully finds and reunites with him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eve is worried that Chance is lost or in danger.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eve finds Chance and they depart together.
